questions.question: In which situation is private IPv4 addressing appropriate for a new subnet on the network of an organization?

options.A: There is limited unique address space, and traffic on the new subnet will stay local within the organization.
options.B: The network has multiple endpoint listeners, and it is desired to limit the number of broadcasts.
options.C: Traffic on the subnet must traverse a site-to-site VPN to an outside organization.
options.D: The ISP requires the new subnet to be advertised to the internet for web services.

questions.question: How do servers connect to the network in a virtual environment?

options.A: wireless to an access point that is physically connected to the network
options.B: a cable connected to a physical switch on the network
options.C: a virtual switch that links to an access point that is physically connected to the network
options.D: a software switch on a hypervisor that is physically connected to the network

questions.question: What is the maximum bandwidth of a T1 point-to-point connection?

options.A: 1.544 Mbps
options.B: 2.048 Mbps
options.C: 34.368 Mbps
options.D: 43.7 Mbps

questions.question: What is a characteristic of a SOHO network?

options.A: connects each switch to every other switch in the network
options.B: enables multiple users to share a single broadband connection
options.C: provides high throughput access for 1000 or more users
options.D: includes at least three tiers of devices to provide load balancing and redundancy

questions.question: Which resource is able to be shared among virtual machines deployed on the same physical server?

options.A: applications
options.B: operating system
options.C: VM configuration file
options.D: disk

questions.question: Which WAN topology provides a combination of simplicity quality, and availability?

options.A: partial mesh
options.B: full mesh
options.C: point-to-point
options.D: hub-and-spoke

questions.question: What is a characteristic of private IPv4 addressing?

options.A: used without tracking or registration
options.B: issued by IANA in conjunction with an autonomous system number
options.C: traverse the Internet when an outbound ACL is applied
options.D: composed of up to 65,536 available addresses

questions.question: What is a similarity between OM3 and OM4 fiber optic cable?

options.A: Both have a 50 micron core diameter
options.B: Both have a 9 micron core diameter
options.C: Both have a 62.5 micron core diameter
options.D: Both have a 100 micron core diameter

questions.question: What describes the operation of virtual machines?

options.A: Virtual machines are responsible for managing and allocating host hardware resources
options.B: Virtual machines are operating system instances that are decoupled from server hardware
options.C: Virtual machines are the physical hardware that support a virtual environment
options.D: In a virtual machine environment, physical servers must run one operating system at a time

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
SiteA#show interface TenGigabitEthernet0/1/0
TenGigabitEthernet0/1/0 is up, line protocol is up
Hardware is BUILT-IN-EPA-8x10G, address is aabb.cc00.0100 (bia aabb.cc00.0100)
Description: Connection to SiteB
Internet address is 10.10.10.1/30
MTU 8146 bytes, BW 10000000 Kbit/sec, DLY 10 usec,
reliability 166/255, txload 1/255, rxload 1/255
Full Duplex, 10000Mbps, link type is force-up, media type is SFP-LR
5 minute input rate 265746000 bits/sec, 24343 packets/sec
5 minute output rate 123245000 bits/sec, 12453 packets/sec
SiteB#show interface TenGigabitEthernet0/1/0
TenGigabitEthernet0/1/0 is up, line protocol is up
Hardware is BUILT-IN-EPA-8x10G, address is 0000.0c00.750c (bia 0000.0c00.750c)
Description: Connection to SiteA
Internet address is 10.10.10.2/30
MTU 8146 bytes, BW 10000000 Kbit/sec, DLY 10 usec,
reliability 255/255, txload 1/255, rxload 1/255
Full Duplex, 10000Mbps, link type is force-up, media type is SFP-LR
5 minute input rate 123245000 bits/sec, 15343 packets/sec
5 minute output rate 265746000 bits/sec, 12453 packets/sec
Shortly after SiteA was connected to SiteB over a new single-mode fiber path, users at SiteA report intermittent connectivity issues with applications hosted at SiteB. What is the cause of the intermittent connectivity issue?

options.A: Interface errors are incrementing
options.B: An incorrect SFP media type was used at SiteA
options.C: High usage is causing high latency
options.D: The sites were connected with the wrong cable type

questions.question: Which technology allows for multiple operating systems to be run on a single host computer?

options.A: virtual device contexts
options.B: network port ID visualization
options.C: virtual routing and forwarding
options.D: server virtualization

questions.question: What is the function of a hub-and-spoke WAN topology?

options.A: supports application optimization
options.B: provides direct connections between subscribers
options.C: supports Layer 2 VPNs
options.D: allows access restrictions to be implemented between subscriber sites

questions.question: What is a characteristic of cloud-based network topology?

options.A: physical workstations are configured to share resources
options.B: services are provided by a public, private, or hybrid deployment
options.C: onsite network services are provided with physical Layer 2 and Layer 3 components
options.D: wireless connections provide the sole access method to services

questions.question: Why was the RFC 1918 address space defined?

options.A: preserve public IPv6 address space
options.B: support the NAT protocol
options.C: reduce instances of overlapping IP addresses
options.D: conserve public IPv4 addressing

questions.question: Which action does the router take as it forwards a packet through the network?

options.A: The router replaces the source and destination labels with the sending router interface label as a source and the next hop router label as a destination
options.B: The router encapsulates the source and destination IP addresses with the sending router IP address as the source and the neighbor IP address as the destination
options.C: The router encapsulates the original packet and then includes a tag that identifies the source router MAC address and transmit transparently to the destination
options.D: The router replaces the original source and destination MAC addresses with the sending router MAC address as the source and neighbor MAC address as the destination

questions.question: An implementer is preparing hardware for virtualization to create virtual machines on a host. What is needed to provide communication between hardware and virtual machines?

options.A: straight cable
options.B: router
options.C: hypervisor
options.D: switch

questions.question: What are two characteristics of the distribution layer in a three-tier network architecture? (Choose two)

options.A: provides a boundary between Layer 2 and Layer 3 communications
options.B: designed to meet continuous, redundant uptime requirements
options.C: serves as the network aggregation point
options.D: physical connection point for a LAN printer
options.E: is the backbone for the network topology

questions.question: What is the difference in data transmission delivery and reliability between TCP and UDP?

options.A: UDP sets up a connection between both devices before transmitting data. TCP uses the three-way handshake to transmit data with a reliable connection.
options.B: TCP transmits data at a higher rate and ensures packet delivery. UDP retransmits lost data to ensure applications receive the data on the remote end.
options.C: UDP is used for multicast and broadcast communication. TCP is used for unicast communication and transmits data at a higher rate with error checking.
options.D: TCP requires the connection to be established before transmitting data. UDP transmits data at a higher rate without ensuring packet delivery.

questions.question: What are network endpoints?

options.A: a threat to the network if they are compromised
options.B: support inter-VLAN connectivity
options.C: act as routers to connect a user to the service prowler network
options.D: enforce policies for campus-wide traffic going to the internet

questions.question: What are two similarities between UTP Cat 5e and Cat 6a cabling? (Choose two)

options.A: Both support runs of up to 100 meters.
options.B: Both support runs of up to 55 meters.
options.C: Both operate at a frequency of 500 MHz.
options.D: Both support speeds of at least 1 Gigabit.
options.E: Both support speeds up to 10 Gigabit.

questions.question: What is a similarly between 1000BASE-LX and 1000BASE-T standards?

options.A: Both use the same data-link header and trailer formats
options.B: Both cable types support LP connectors
options.C: Both cable types support RJ-45 connectors
options.D: Both support up to 550 meters between nodes

questions.question: What are two characteristics of a public cloud implementation? (Choose two)

options.A: It is owned and maintained by one party, but it is shared among multiple organizations
options.B: It enables an organization to fully customize how it deploys network resources
options.C: It provides services that are accessed over the Internet
options.D: It is a data center on the public Internet that maintains cloud services for only one company
options.E: It supports network resources from a centralized third-party provider and privately-owned virtual resources

questions.question: Which type of organization should use a collapsed-core architecture?

options.A: large and requires a flexible, scalable network design
options.B: small and needs to reduce networking costs currently
options.C: large and must minimize downtime when hardware fails
options.D: small but is expected to grow dramatically in the near future

questions.question: What is an appropriate use for private IPv4 addressing?

options.A: on the public-facing interface of a firewall
options.B: to allow hosts inside to communicate in both directions with hosts outside the organization
options.C: on internal hosts that stream data solely to external resources
options.D: on hosts that communicates only with other internal hosts

Shortly after SiteA was connected to SiteB over a new single-mode fiber path, users at SiteA report intermittent connectivity issues with applications hosted at SiteB. What is the cause of the intermittent connectivity issue?

options.A: An incorrect type of transceiver has been inserted into a device on the link.
options.B: The wrong cable type was used to make the connection.
options.C: Heavy usage is causing high latency.
options.D: Physical network errors are being transmitted between the two sites.

questions.question: What is a characteristic or private IPv4 addressing?

options.A: composed of up to 65,536 available addresses
options.B: issued by IANA in conjunction with an autonomous system number
options.C: used without tracking or registration
options.D: traverse the Internet when an outbound ACL is applied

questions.question: What is a function of an endpoint on a network?

options.A: allows users to record data and transmit to a file server
options.B: connects server and client devices to a network
options.C: provides wireless services to users in a building
options.D: forwards traffic between VLANs on a network

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
Traffic that is flowing over interface TenGigabitEthernet0/0 experiences slow transfer speeds. What is the reason for the issue?

options.A: heavy traffic congestion
options.B: queuing drops
options.C: a speed conflict
options.D: a duplex incompatibility

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
What is a reason for poor performance on the network interface?

options.A: The interface is receiving excessive broadcast traffic.
options.B: The cable connection between the two devices is faulty.
options.C: The interface is operating at a different speed than the connected device.
options.D: The bandwidth setting of the interface is misconfigured

questions.question: How do UTP and STP cables compare?

options.A: STP cables are cheaper to produre and easier to install and UTP cables are more expensive and harder to install.
options.B: UTP cables are less prone to crosstalk and interference and STP cables are more prone to crosstalk and interference.
options.C: UTP cables provide faster and more reliable data transfer rates and STP cables are slower and less reliable.
options.D: STP cables are shielded and protect against electromagnetic interference and UTP lacks the same protection against electromagnetic interference.

questions.question: Which WAN topology has the highest degree of reliability?

options.A: router-on-a-stick
options.B: Point-to-point
options.C: hub-and-spoke
options.D: full mesh

questions.question: What is a benefit for external users who consume public cloud resources?

options.A: implemented over a dedicated WAN
options.B: located in the same data center as the users
options.C: all hosted on physical servers
options.D: accessed over the Internet

questions.question: When should an engineer implement a collapsed-core architecture?

options.A: for large networks that are connected to multiple remote sites
options.B: the access and distribution layers must be on the same device
options.C: only when using VSS technology
options.D: for small networks with minimal need for growth

questions.question: What is the difference between 1000BASE-LX/LH and 1000BASE-ZX interfaces?

options.A: 1000BASE-LX/LH interoperates with multimode and single-mode fiber, and 1000BASE-ZX needs a conditioning patch cable with a multimode.
options.B: 1000BASE-ZX is supported on links up to 1000km, and 1000BASE-LX/LH operates over links up to 70 km.
options.C: 1000BASE-ZX interoperates with dual-rate 100M/1G 10Km SFP over multimode fiber, and 1000BASE-LX/LH supports only single-rate.
options.D: 1000BASE-LX/LH is supported on links up to 10km, and 1000BASE-ZX operates over links up to 70 km.

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
What is expected when PC_A sends data to PC_B after their initial communication?

options.A: The source and destination MAC addresses remain the same
options.B: The switch rewrites the source and destination MAC addresses with its own
options.C: The source MAC address is changed
options.D: The destination MAC address is replaced with ffff.ffff.ffff

questions.question: What is the collapsed layer in collapsed core architectures?

options.A: core and WAN
options.B: access and WAN
options.C: distribution and access
options.D: core and distribution

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
The given Windows PC is requesting the IP address of the host at www.cisco.com. To which IP address is the request sent?
What is a function of an endpoint?

options.A: It passes unicast communication between hosts in a network
options.B: It is used directly by an individual user to access network services
options.C: It provides security between trusted and untrusted sections of the network
options.D: It transmits broadcast traffic between devices in the same VLAN

questions.question: In a cloud-computing environment, what is rapid elasticity?

options.A: automatic adjustment of capacity based on need
options.B: control and monitoring of resource consumption by the tenant
options.C: pooling resources in a multitenant model based on need
options.D: self-service of computing resources by the tenant

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
A network engineer must configure the link with these requirements:
• Consume as few IP addresses as possible.
• Leave at least two additional useable IP addresses for future growth.
Which set of configurations must be applied?
How do TCP and UDP fit into a query-response model?

options.A: TCP avoids using sequencing, and UDP avoids using acknowledgments.
options.B: TCP uses error detection for packets, and UDP uses error recovery.
options.C: TCP establishes a connection prior to sending data, and UDP sends immediately.
options.D: TCP encourages out-of-order packet delivery, and UDP prevents re-ordering.

questions.question: What are two reasons to implement IPv4 private addressing on a network? (Choose two)

options.A: to expand the routing table on the router
options.B: to facilitate renumbering when merging networks
options.C: to enable internal applications to treat the private IPv4 addresses as unique
options.D: to conserve global unique IPv4 addresses
options.E: to provide protection from external denial-of-service attacks
